executive_summary.recommendation: NUCLEAR RESET REQUIRED - Delete and start over with simplicity

ultrathink.md.savage_commentary: This isn't thinking, it's bureaucracy. 356 lines to wrap Claude's native thinking? That's like building a 47-story office building to house a single calculator.
failure_patterns: XML ceremony for sake of ceremony
Violates every simplicity principle in CLAUDE.md

analyze-project-architecture.md.savage_commentary: 273 lines to say 'use Glob, Grep, and Read tools'? I've seen wedding planning documents with better efficiency ratios.
failure_patterns: Multi-phase architecture for basic file operations
Over-engineered discovery strategy for simple directory traversal
analyze-project-architecture.md.actual_value: MARGINAL - Basic tools would work better

ultrathink-full-mcp.md.savage_commentary: This is the USS Enterprise of command prompts. 664 lines, 12 MCP tools, and enough complexity to make NASA engineers weep. This isn't a command, it's a small operating system that accidentally learned to talk.
failure_patterns: Agent orchestration for problems that don't need agents
Sequential thinking wrapper around native thinking

ultrathink-pure-xml.md.savage_commentary: 471 lines of XML theater to avoid MCP dependencies, only to reinvent every single pattern they were trying to avoid. It's like rejecting a car because of gas dependency, then building a manual sedan from scratch.
ultrathink-pure-xml.md.actual_value: QUESTIONABLE - No clear benefit over direct asking

adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.savage_commentary: 456 lines to set a timer and take breaks. This is the productivity equivalent of using a forklift to crack a walnut. The irony of creating complexity to manage ADHD hyperfocus is chef's kiss perfect.
failure_patterns: JavaScript pattern complexity for timer management
7-step process to start a 90-minute focus session
Basic Memory dependency for session tracking
adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.actual_value: LIMITED - Phone timer + discipline more effective

savage_final_verdict.bottom_line: THESE COMMANDS ARE THE SOFTWARE ENGINEERING EQUIVALENT OF RUBE GOLDBERG MACHINES
brutal_truth: A simple '/user:explain X' would outperform every single one of these commands
The complexity added provides zero measurable benefit
These commands violate every principle in their own CLAUDE.md
The irony is stunning: commands about thinking that show no evidence of thinking
This is abstraction without implementation, pattern without problem

immediate_actions: DELETE any command with >1 MCP dependency for simple tasks
START OVER with CLAUDE.md simplicity principles
quality_gates: Every command MUST have complexity score <5
Every command MUST solve a REAL problem
Every command MUST be simpler than alternatives

statistical_confidence.validity: HIGH - Results consistent across all samples

peer_review_challenge.criteria: Based on project's own CLAUDE.md standards
